channel.title: Programming on LiaScript
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on LiaScript

item.title: AVR8js-mem for LiaScript: Memory Register Visualization for AVR Microcontrollers
item.description: The AVR8js template lets you run Arduino C++ code in the browser with Wokwi components. AVR8js-mem by Fabian Bär extends it with a key teaching feature: real-time memory register visualization. After each simulation cycle, you can display the value of any memory register in binary, hexadecimal, unsigned integer, or custom format — and even plot the values over time as a live diagram.

item.title: BiwaScheme for LiaScript: Functional Programming with Scheme in the Browser
item.description: Scheme is one of the most influential programming languages ever designed. Its minimal syntax, powerful macro system, and emphasis on functional programming have made it a foundational language in computer science education — from SICP to modern functional programming courses.

item.title: Curiosity-Prolog for LiaScript: Lightweight Logic Programming in the Browser
item.description: Not every logic programming exercise needs a full Prolog system. For introductory exercises — family trees, simple rule-based reasoning, first-order logic — a lighter interpreter is often more appropriate.

item.title: Custom Code Imports for LiaScript: Load External Files Into Executable Blocks
item.description: LiaScript’s code blocks are normally written directly in the Markdown source. The Custom-code-imports playground by André Dietrich demonstrates a powerful pattern: fetching the content of an external source file by URL and injecting it as a live, executable LiaScript code block at runtime. This is particularly useful for courses that should always show the latest version of a codebase, or for exercises that load student submissions for review.

item.title: JSCPP for LiaScript: Run C++ Directly in the Browser Without a Compiler
item.description: C++ programming courses have a tooling problem. Students need to install a compiler, configure an IDE, manage build systems, and debug their environment before writing a single line of code.

item.title: Lua for LiaScript: Execute Lua Scripts Interactively in the Browser
item.description: Lua is lightweight, fast, embeddable, and widely used — in game development (Roblox, World of Warcraft, Löve2D), embedded systems, configuration scripting, and teaching. Its simple syntax and minimal runtime make it an excellent first language.

item.title: p5js for LiaScript: Creative Coding and Generative Graphics in the Browser
item.description: p5.js brings the Processing creative coding environment to the web. It is built for artists, designers, educators, and beginners who want to draw, animate, and interact with graphics using JavaScript.

item.title: Tau-Prolog for LiaScript: Interactive Logic Programming in the Browser
item.description: Prolog is the language of logic programming. Unlike imperative languages, Prolog programs describe what is true rather than how to compute it. This makes it essential in AI, natural language processing, theorem proving, and formal methods courses.

item.title: TinyTurtle for LiaScript: Teach JavaScript with Interactive Turtle Graphics
item.description: Turtle graphics is one of the most effective ways to introduce programming. The immediate visual feedback — a turtle drawing lines on screen — connects commands to results in a way that abstract code examples cannot.

item.title: CodeRunner for LiaScript: Execute Code in 50+ Languages Server-Side
item.description: Not every programming language runs in a browser. Browser-based interpreters like Pyodide cover Python beautifully, but if your course needs real C compilation, JVM-based Java, Rust, Haskell, Erlang, or even COBOL — you need a real server with real compilers.

item.title: MicroBit-Simulator for LiaScript: Teach MicroPython Without a Physical Device
item.description: The BBC micro:bit is one of the most popular physical computing boards in schools worldwide. With the MicroBit-Simulator template, you can bring it directly into your LiaScript course — no hardware, no installation, no classroom set of devices needed.

item.title: Pyodide for LiaScript: Run Python Directly in Your Course
item.description: Python is the most widely taught programming language in the world. With the Pyodide template, you can bring it directly into your LiaScript course — executable, editable, and fully browser-based.

item.title: Lua Programming
item.description: This example is a port of the Lua programming course from the open wikibook Lua-Programming to LiaScript. It is a fully working Lua programming course, that can be used as a basis for your own course.

item.title: How To Code in Python 3
item.description: This is a port of DigitalOcean’s How To Code in Python 3 tutorial series by Lisa Tagliaferri translated to LiaScript with interactive PyScript examples.

item.title: Arbeitsbuch PROLOG (German)
item.description: Dieses Buch soll in die Programmiersprache PROLOG und in die mit ihr verbundene ‘Logik-programmierung’ einführen. Die Idee, ‘Logik als eine Programmiersprache’ zu verwenden, führt zu einer völlig neuen Auffassung vom Programmieren: Das Programm ist nicht eine Folge von Handlungsanweisungen, sondern eine Sammlung von Fakten und Regeln. Das zu lösende Problem wird als eine Anfrage an diese Sammlung formuliert. Im Idealfall führt die logisch korrekte Formulierung der Regeln und der Anfrage zur Lösung des Problems.

item.title: C-Programmierung (German)
item.description: Dies ist eine interaktive Portierung des freien Wikibuches C-Programmierung nach LiaScript.

item.title: C-Programming
item.description: This LiaScript book is based on the free Wikibook on C-Programming. It provides a comprehensive look at the C programming language and its features. Examples are made interactive by using the CodeRunner.
